title: $shift and $shiftx ignore \A_SIGNED
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

```
$ cat /tmp/test.il
module \shifttest
  wire width 8 output 1 \Y_shift
  wire width 8 output 2 \Y_shr

  cell $shift \UUT_shift
    parameter \A_SIGNED 1
    parameter \A_WIDTH 4
    parameter \B_SIGNED 0
    parameter \B_WIDTH 4
    parameter \Y_WIDTH 8
    connect \A 4'1010
    connect \B 4'0001
    connect \Y \Y_shift
  end

  cell $shr \UUT_shr
    parameter \A_SIGNED 1
    parameter \A_WIDTH 4
    parameter \B_SIGNED 0
    parameter \B_WIDTH 4
    parameter \Y_WIDTH 8
    connect \A 4'1010
    connect \B 4'0001
    connect \Y \Y_shr
  end
end
$ ./yosys -p 'check; eval' /tmp/test.il

 /----------------------------------------------------------------------------\
 |                                                                            |
 |  yosys -- Yosys Open SYnthesis Suite                                       |
 |                                                                            |
 |  Copyright (C) 2012 - 2020  Claire Wolf <claire@symbioticeda.com>          |

 |                                                                            |
 \----------------------------------------------------------------------------/

 Yosys 0.9+2406 (git sha1 b822beb1, gcc 10.1.0 -fPIC -Os)


-- Parsing `/tmp/test.il' using frontend `ilang' --

1. Executing ILANG frontend.
Input filename: /tmp/test.il

-- Running command `check; eval' --

2. Executing CHECK pass (checking for obvious problems).
checking module shifttest..
found and reported 0 problems.

3. Executing EVAL pass (evaluate the circuit given an input).
Eval result: \Y_shr = 8'01111101.
Eval result: \Y_shift = 8'00000101.

End of script. Logfile hash: 47cd0e26f9, CPU: user 0.01s system 0.00s, MEM: 16.33 MB peak
Yosys 0.9+2406 (git sha1 b822beb1, gcc 10.1.0 -fPIC -Os)
Time spent: 49% 1x eval (0 sec), 27% 1x read_ilang (0 sec), ...
```

## Expected behavior

`$shift` behaves the same as `$shr` for positive/unsigned `B` operands.

## Actual behavior

`$shr` sign-extends its `A` operand according to `\A_SIGNED` before shifting, `$shift` always zero-extends.

If this is the desired behaviour, `check` should error on an asserted `\A_SIGNED` for `$shift`/`$shiftx`, since it cannot possibly have the desired effect.

  wire width 8 output 2 \Y_shr

    parameter \B_WIDTH 4
